metadata.task: Add a new container hash maps bench variant to HashMapsBench.cpp for the container_hash_maps_bench binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264] benchmark and nearby variants such as Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11], %InsertGrow   f14vec<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264]. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase block_size_P50 substantially relative to Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264]. Current baseline for Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264]: block_size_P50=5.

Add a new container hash maps bench variant to HashMapsBench.cpp for the container_hash_maps_bench binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264] benchmark and nearby variants such as Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11], %InsertGrow   f14vec<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264]. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase family::load_P50 with a large change relative to Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264]. Target delta for family::load_P50: about +1.125 with acceptable range >= +0.9. Current baseline for Clear  f14node<NonSSOString, a[128]>[11264]: family::load_P50=0.

Add a new container hash maps bench variant to HashMapsBench.cpp for the container_hash_maps_bench binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing %Clear   f14val<NonSSOString, a[128]>[352] benchmark and nearby variants such as %Find   f14val<NonSSOString, a[128]>[352], %Dtor   f14val<uint64_t, a[128]>[11264]. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should decrease branch-load-misses_MPKI with a small change relative to %Clear   f14val<NonSSOString, a[128]>[352]. Target delta for branch-load-misses_MPKI: about -0.419763 with acceptable range -0.67162 to -0.167905. Current baseline for %Clear   f14val<NonSSOString, a[128]>[352]: branch-load-misses_MPKI=0.420209.

Add a new fibers fibers benchmark variant to FibersBenchmark.cpp for the fibers_fibers_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern benchmark and nearby variants such as FiberManagerAllocateLargeChunk, FiberManagerCancelledTimeouts_Single_300. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should decrease branch-load-misses_MPKI with a large change relative to FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern. Target delta for branch-load-misses_MPKI: about -0.400382 with acceptable range <= -0.333651. Current baseline for FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern: branch-load-misses_MPKI=0.928627. To reduce branch-load misses, keep the hot branch footprint compact and repeatedly use a smaller set of branch sites and jump targets. Prefer simpler control flow, fewer hot dispatch targets, and less bouncing across distant hot regions of code.

Add a new fibers fibers benchmark variant to FibersBenchmark.cpp for the fibers_fibers_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern benchmark and nearby variants such as FiberManagerAllocateLargeChunk, FiberManagerCancelledTimeouts_Single_300. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should decrease branch-misses_MPKI with a large change relative to FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern. Target delta for branch-misses_MPKI: about -0.177422 with acceptable range <= -0.147851. Current baseline for FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern: branch-misses_MPKI=1.15097. To reduce branch misses, make the common path obvious, repetitive, and easy for the branch predictor to learn. Prefer stable hot control flow with predictable taken/not-taken behavior and fewer data-dependent surprises on the steady-state path.

Add a new fibers fibers benchmark variant to FibersBenchmark.cpp for the fibers_fibers_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern benchmark and nearby variants such as FiberManagerAllocateLargeChunk, FiberManagerCancelledTimeouts_Single_300. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase IPC with a large change relative to FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern. Target delta for IPC: about +0.564854 with acceptable range >= +0.470711. Current baseline for FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern: IPC=2.1.

Add a new fibers fibers benchmark variant to FibersBenchmark.cpp for the fibers_fibers_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern benchmark and nearby variants such as FiberManagerAllocateLargeChunk, FiberManagerCancelledTimeouts_Single_300. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase L1-dcache-loads_LPKI with a medium change relative to FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern. Target delta for L1-dcache-loads_LPKI: about +54.7087 with acceptable range +33.6669 to +75.7505. Current baseline for FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern: L1-dcache-loads_LPKI=247.245. To increase L1-dcache-loads_LPKI, perform more hot-path reads per unit of work and rely less on reusing values already held in registers. Prefer repeated reloading of memory-resident values, more read traffic through arrays or structures, and less register reuse across iterations. Avoid changes that mostly add control-flow complexity without increasing steady-state read traffic.

Add a new fibers fibers benchmark variant to FibersBenchmark.cpp for the fibers_fibers_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern benchmark and nearby variants such as FiberManagerAllocateLargeChunk, FiberManagerCancelledTimeouts_Single_300. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase L1-dcache-load-misses_MPKI with a medium change relative to FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern. Target delta for L1-dcache-load-misses_MPKI: about +2.25889 with acceptable range +1.39009 to +3.12769. Current baseline for FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern: L1-dcache-load-misses_MPKI=0.051221. To increase L1-dcache miss rate, grow the repeatedly accessed data footprint beyond what fits comfortably in L1D and keep that larger footprint on the hot path. Prefer loop bodies that touch more distinct cache lines per unit of work, with weaker short-term reuse and less spatial or temporal locality. Avoid changes that only add code complexity while keeping the same compact hot data set; that rarely moves L1-dcache misses.

Add a new fibers fibers benchmark variant to FibersBenchmark.cpp for the fibers_fibers_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern benchmark and nearby variants such as FiberManagerAllocateLargeChunk, FiberManagerCancelledTimeouts_Single_300. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase L1-dcache-stores_SPKI with a large change relative to FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern. Target delta for L1-dcache-stores_SPKI: about +15.8232 with acceptable range >= +12.6585. Current baseline for FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern: L1-dcache-stores_SPKI=152.16. To increase L1-dcache-stores_SPKI, perform more hot-path writes per unit of work and materialize more temporaries back to memory. Prefer code that writes intermediate results more often instead of keeping them live in registers for longer. Avoid one-time setup writes; the steady-state loop must repeatedly generate the additional store traffic.

Add a new fibers fibers benchmark variant to FibersBenchmark.cpp for the fibers_fibers_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern benchmark and nearby variants such as FiberManagerAllocateLargeChunk, FiberManagerCancelledTimeouts_Single_300. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase L1-icache-load-misses_MPKI with a large change relative to FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern. Target delta for L1-icache-load-misses_MPKI: about +11.7403 with acceptable range >= +9.7836. Current baseline for FiberManagerAllocateDeallocatePattern: L1-icache-load-misses_MPKI=0.275295. Do not rely on a single larger helper or template expansion by itself; bigger code only helps if the frequently executed instruction working set grows enough to pressure the instruction cache. Put the added footprint on the hot path. Favor several distinct noinline hot helpers or dispatch targets that are all exercised repeatedly inside the benchmark loop, so the CPU must fetch across a materially larger executed code footprint. Avoid adding mostly cold setup logic, rare slow paths, or one-time initialization. Those increase binary size without materially increasing instruction-cache misses. Prefer designs that alternate among multiple hot code bodies with genuinely different instruction sequences instead of repeatedly executing one compact loop body.

Add a new function benchmark variant to main.cpp for the function_benchmark binary. Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing catch_no_exception benchmark and nearby variants such as throw_exception, Function_create_move_invoke_small. Do not delete, rename, or weaken existing benchmarks; preserve the existing benchmark harness and main() unless a build fix absolutely requires a local adjustment. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase branch-load-misses_MPKI with a medium change relative to catch_no_exception. Target delta for branch-load-misses_MPKI: about +0.481208 with acceptable range +0.374065 to +0.675289. Current baseline for catch_no_exception: branch-load-misses_MPKI=0.053865. To increase branch-load misses, stress the branch target structures and front-end by using a larger hot set of branch sites and jump targets. Prefer hot execution that bounces among many call targets, dispatch targets, or branch sites rather than repeatedly taking the same small set of branches. Inlining or expanding code can help only if it materially increases the hot branch footprint; a huge cold body or one-time setup path is usually not enough. Interpreter-like dispatch, many distinct indirect or conditional branch targets, and movement across separated hot regions are more effective than one compact loop.

The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase branch-misses_MPKI with a large change relative to catch_no_exception. Target delta for branch-misses_MPKI: about +0.976662 with acceptable range >= +0.713234. Current baseline for catch_no_exception: branch-misses_MPKI=0.055199. To increase branch misses, make hot-path decisions depend on unpredictable input so the branch predictor cannot learn a simple repetitive pattern. Prefer data-dependent conditionals whose taken/not-taken behavior varies irregularly across iterations instead of obvious repetitive control flow. Avoid only bloating code size; branch-misses primarily move when the predictor sees less predictable decisions, not merely more instructions.

Target delta for L1-dcache-load-misses_MPKI: about -0.0525853 with acceptable range -0.0788779 to -0.05. Current baseline for catch_no_exception: L1-dcache-load-misses_MPKI=0.075011. To reduce L1-dcache miss rate, make the hot data working set smaller and more reusable so frequently accessed data stays resident in L1D. Prefer tighter locality, more repeated access to a compact hot set, and fewer cache-line touches per unit of work.

Keep the coding style and benchmark registration patterns consistent with the existing catch_no_exception benchmark and nearby variants such as throw_exception, Function_create_move_invoke_small. The new variant should appear as an additional microbenchmark in --bm_list and should increase L1-dcache-loads_LPKI with a large change relative to catch_no_exception. Target delta for L1-dcache-loads_LPKI: about +0.856063 with acceptable range >= +0.713386. Current baseline for catch_no_exception: L1-dcache-loads_LPKI=171.904. To increase L1-dcache-loads_LPKI, perform more hot-path reads per unit of work and rely less on reusing values already held in registers. Prefer repeated reloading of memory-resident values, more read traffic through arrays or structures, and less register reuse across iterations. Avoid changes that mostly add control-flow complexity without increasing steady-state read traffic.
